title: Chicago Luxury Rentals: Neighborhood-by-Neighborhood Guide
dek: Chicago's luxury rental market isn't one market. It's a collection of neighborhoods with distinct personalities, price logics, and renter types. After ten years leasing here, here's the honest map. (Price ranges shift monthly; treat these as relative tiers and check current numbers.)

blocks.html: The default landing zone for young professionals and first-time relocators: high-rise density, nightlife, galleries, walk-to-Loop. Buildings compete hard on amenities, which means strong concession seasons for savvy renters. Renter type: the young professional, overwhelmingly.

Lake-adjacent, hospital- and Northwestern-anchored, calmer than River North with equal convenience. Draws relocating medical and corporate professionals who want proximity without the party. Renter type: the relocating executive.

The Loop delivers commute-zero living and conversions with character; South Loop trades a short distance for space, newer towers, and relative value, plus museum-campus and lakefront access. Renter types: executives in the Loop, value-conscious professionals and couples in South Loop.

The decade's momentum story: restaurant row, tech and corporate offices, new construction everywhere. Premium pricing and renters who want to be where Chicago is going, not where it's been. Renter type: young professionals with real budgets, plus executives at nearby headquarters.

Legacy luxury: tree-lined prestige, boutique buildings, lake proximity, quieter money. Old Town softens the formality with charm and Second City energy. Renter type: the lifestyle upgrader, plus executives who prefer establishment over energy.

Neighborhood luxury rather than tower luxury: parks, the zoo, brownstone streets, family-friendly rhythm with serious rental stock mixed in. Renter type: upgraders and settled professionals planting longer roots.

blocks.text: How an agent should read this map

blocks.html: Each neighborhood is a renter-type magnet, which means your Power 25 list should match the clients you want. Build in River North and West Loop for volume and young professionals; add Streeterville and the Gold Coast for executives and upgraders. The neighborhoods are the market. Learn them like inventory.
Relocating to Chicago, or an agent building here? My free ebook Luxury Leases, Real Profits covers the system side, and I write about this market every Saturday in The Lease Up. Better agents win.
